reviews.review_title: You’ve come a long way, baby
reviews.review_description:
Bought this early doors in early access and tried it only a couple of times in the last 2 years because it was too rough around the edges.
Loaded it up again yesterday and -WOW- what a transformation in the past year. In less than 24 hours I have drained my Quest 3 (with battery strap) twice playing this game, and have now reached level 8.
There are not enough racing games on standalone Quest, but at least this one is a cracker!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the trust in our game! We're glad it met your expectations. We hope you'll look forward the next major updates with new cars and maps!

reviews.review_title: Great racing game, just needs more....
reviews.review_description:
We need more cars and maps.
The city setting is fine but I feel what could just make it feel a little less repetitive and give a bit more charm to the city is to have the barriers not be a solid red colour all the way around. Maybe some advertisements just fake ones and cones to accidentally hit and the odd set of tyres to give character to each different track layout. 

More cars are a must also. Hatchbacks are fine but we need to have something to grind for. 

Apart from new maps as well as cars I think everything is there.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Now that Early Access is over we will focus on new content like new maps and cars, you can check the roadmap in the Extras menu to see what's coming next. We also want to add some extra details to the Downtown scene!

reviews.review_description:
This racing game is such a great game. For me, it’s the best racing game so far on Quest 3. It’s one I keep coming back to, even though other racing games have been released since, I still prefer this one.

The graphics and level of detail are impressive, considering how well it runs on a standalone Quest 3. Mirrors, lighting at different times of day, reflections, all add to the realism. With some buildings, you can even look inside the rooms, which have actual depth.

But most importantly, the driving feels just right.

There are also plenty of comfort and control options. For example, I lowered the steering wheel position so I can rest my arms more naturally. 

The haptic feedback in the controllers is also very convincing, you can even feel it when driving over road markings. You can choose between automatic or manual shifting. With automatic, you can actually see the gear stick moving. There is also a handbreak. 

Multiplayer is a lot of fun, and voice chat works well. Driving in the rain adds an extra challenge and makes the races even more exciting.

It’s especially impressive that this was made almost entirely by one single developer. Definitely worth checking out!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the wonderful review! We hope you’ll continue coming to the game in the future with new updates!

reviews.review_title: Will drift be added in future versions?
reviews.review_description:
I learned about this game from Vspeedway  and really enjoy the feeling of drifting in VR.But In DTC, because it's all front wheel drive cars, it's almost difficult to drift. Although it simulates reality, it lacks a lot of excitement for me. 

I wonder if drifting will be added in future versions? This is really important to me. And if u read the comments , u will find that  it is a common demand for most players.

developer_response.body:
We announced and revealed drift cars quite some time ago, they are the next thing on the roadmap after the Full Launch on June 11th! You can see the details in the in-game newsfeed, in our socials, and in the roadmap accessible from the "Extra" menu. 

We always reads all the comments, but do players read our announcements? :D

reviews.review_title: Great racing game just getting started!
reviews.review_description:
This Dev team knows how to make a great physics style racer.  From V speedway to this they took what works best and vastly improved it.
This is the best car racing Sim on Meta by far.  Can't wait for future updates.  Great job!

Update 6/12/26:  I got this game when it first dropped into early access and even then it was the best available on the Meta store.  As of today I still stand by that statement.  It's not quite a full simulation or an arcade racer.  I would describe it as a simcade leaning heavier towards simulation.  What they pulled off on standalone is amazing.  From working mirrors to how you can grip the steering wheel how you want.  The AI racer's in single player act and react as if it's an experienced human opponent.  My only gripe was that it needed more! More tracks and cars. That will come though.  This solo dev is completely committed to this passion project and I hope this is a success so he can continue to add to an already amazing racer.  Worth every penny and some!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for sticking with the game, really appreciate that! With Early Access now out of the way, all next updates are focusing on new cars and maps, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: I've been following the development of this game ever since i heard about it, and its state today is a very polished VR Racer, with very well executed virtual wheel steering and driving interaction with multiplayer to boot. If you want the best, we'll..  then this is the one to get.

I like this game, it works good (never had glitch or something). But only 1 map,for now, with multiple different type of races available, but, you know, with bots on normal level i am thinking only about win (they are really tough and i like it)

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! After the next update to exit Early Access, all upcoming updates will focus on new cars and maps, so we hope you'll look forward to them!

reviews.review_description:
The game is incredibly addictive and fun, and challenging yet not impossible when driving in the AI Events on Hard mode, but put it up to champion and it’s practically impossible to win the Cup. 

I asked for a harder race, not to have what feels like less grip, to have the AI have 1000 more Horsepower than me, or to have the AI suddenly drive as dirty as possible. 

The amount of times I’m sideswiped on a clean overtake down a straight,is unbearable, and at this point. Find hard mode too easy to win on. Apart from that the only other annoying thing is that the physical gearstick is annoying to use, and feels uncomfortable to maneuver. 

Otherwise the game is again, adictive and incredibly fun to play. And well worth the money.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We are aware the AI needs some work, we're working on some improvements for update v1.4.0 (you can already try a Test version on the dedicated release channel).

reviews.review_description: I love the game I love the physics I especially like these newer physics just a little bored of the map and can’t wait to drift around the new ones

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! We're at work on the new update, it will take some time but we can't wait to release it later on. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_title: Best Simcade on the whole platform! 🙌🔥
reviews.review_description:
V-Speedway was something but now this is just incredible! It has basically everything V-Speedway was lacking lol, although my favorite thing to do was cranking the wheel 900° and drifting on and on (which is not quite possible in Downtown Club) the way cars handle and how you can fully feel your car's weight shifting through corners and even use that weight shift to your advantage completely makes up for it!

And about drifting part, we'll have to wait and see for ourselves when 1.5.0 (JDM Cars Update) comes around the corner 👀‼️

Love you guys' work, this game's awesome!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Even if Downtown Club was born from the codebase of V-Speedeway, it's always been a very different and better game, where we poured every drop of passion and everything we learned along the way. Please look forward to future updates!

reviews.review_title: Best current driving game on vr
reviews.review_description: Singlehandly the best driving game you can get on the quest platform currently. The roadmap shows how much potential this game currently has and even as it is, it is stellar compared to almost everything else. Would be my recommended pick first time every time for its genre.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, that's flattering! We hope you are looking forward to the next updates. Enjoy!

reviews.review_description: I love racing games but was skeptical to purchase this because of the motion sickness!!! I was wrong!!!! It has been a blast so far, the car is easy to control but u can lose control by braking to hard.. that makes the experience edgy and fun.. very impressive so far.once I become a better driver I will attempt the multiplayer mode. For now I’m gonna rule the road in my own little world!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, we're glad you're enjoying this! We have several updates planned next so we hope you'll look forward to them!

reviews.review_description:
Firstly thank you for the first class comfort options, this is the first vr racing game I've been able to live with regarding motion sickness :)

For me this game joins..'in death unchained," and "golf 5" as my goto games.. it's that good!, having spent the last 40 years using various pc/console ffb wheels I've been looking forward to finding a good game to get me into the world of vr racing,

Downtown club is that game... as with all racing games it's all about the handling, and DTC certainly doesn't disappoint in that respect..I find the toggle hands on to steering wheel option really useful, 

I really only have one gripe..I would like more feedback to controller, maybe to the point where the rumble on controller is on permanently..as it should be taking into consideration car and road vibration, 

looking forward to future updates on this one.. great game

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We hope you will look forward to future updates, there's a lot going on behind the scenes.

reviews.review_description: This is an amazing racing game on Stand-Alone VR. There are different cars you can choose from, a customizable driver, multiplayer maps, and smart AI Racers. I have encountered minimal bugs, and the bad ones that i have seen before, have been fixed. You can choose if you want your car to be manual, or automatic, there are real-time shadows, the mirrors work, and they will be adding drifting in future updates. I played V-Speedway when it first released, and this game is a huge step-up. Thank you Devs for making one of, if not, the best racing game on stand-alone VR.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're still working a lot on the game and have plenty of updates to come. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
Yo get the game support devs because this game is really well put together and it’s fun the multiplayer works I’ve encountered players and the game works flawlessly but more people need to play this game especially if you like racing and cars and even just driving. They have all different types of races series’ and map variations and adjustable npc opponent difficulty 
Let’s support these devs and get more people playing this game !!!

p.s. I need more people to race!

developer_response.body: Everyone, listen to this player! :D Thanks a lot, we hope now that multiplayer is out, more people will join to play.

reviews.review_description:
Even though this games states "Internet Connection Not Required" on the Horizon store page, an active internet connection is required for authentication purposes. No internet no play. 
******Update: Bug has been fixed. 
Played the game. Graphics are nice. Love the fact that there's an option to drive manual.
Highly recommend 👌

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the updated review, and sorry again for the bug! Very glad this has worked for you. Hope you'll have a good time in the game!

reviews.review_description: It's the best driving game ive played over the years on my quest and is very actively developed my only genuine criticism is the music is lacking

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the review! We love our old fashioned rock though. :P

reviews.review_title: Most fun i've had in a long time
reviews.review_description:
This game is a solid 5 stars. I have a low budget racing sim (G920) and it doesn't come close to how good this game plays. The driving feels great.

The only things I don't like are that the manual transmission is hard to drive with. I have no problem driving manual but with this game it feels super janky for some reason. In V-Speedway I had no issue with the manual transmission. Maybe it's a skill issue💯

I can't wait to see new cars and maps in the future.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We had various reports that manual transmission is harder to use here and we'll look into it further in an upcoming update. Anyways we have plenty of plans for the game for sure, hope you'll stay for the updates!

reviews.review_description:
Full motion control support and the controls feels so tight and on point. the game looks good and there's tons of nice little features. this game has a lot of potential if the dev keeps going at it!  

I tried a bunch of PCVR racers and didn't like any of them. the lack of motion control support is really a bummer. 

so this is by far my best experience with a VR racer!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! We sure want to continue building on it and add new content!

reviews.review_title: Annoying hand placing on steering wheel
reviews.review_description: Please remove the hand placing feature on the steering wheel when I use handbrakes or gearstick, I like my hands where they are on the steering wheel. What youre doing is not cool and its throwing me off my focus when Im driving. Take it out immediately, as part of your next update please. I promise to give you 5 stars if you do so.

developer_response.body: Sorry, but I will keep the current hand positioning logic. It exists to prevent the hands to accumulate excessive offset every time you release and grab the wheel again, when using the Fixed mode. Please change to Sliding mode if you prefer that.

reviews.review_description:
it’s not perfect by now, but theres a lot of updates and good things going on.
virtual wheel works absolutely fair enough(if you adjust it right to your needs in the settings ,that is)

i have a problem though..i want to drive with manuel gear, but i can’t because, i mostly play it sitting in bed and my choices are wheel or shifting;) because of just one “height” in the car sitting down, i just can reach one or the other..:/
so maybe if we can allign them to our personal cockpit individually that would be great:) also give us all axis on the steering wheel please:)

great game so far, and i love the latest updates

keep it up:)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! I'm not totally sure I understood the issue, feel free to hop in our Discord so we can chat about that and see what I can do! Glad you're enjoying the updates anyways, and we have plenty in the works right now.

reviews.review_description: The game is pretty fun and the driving mechanics are satisfying probably the best racing game on the Quest

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! We're doing our best. :)

reviews.review_description:
Nice graphics, good mechanics, a proper game.

single player is fun, online is great.  excellent job

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, very appreciated! Please look forward to the upcoming updates, cheers!

reviews.review_description:
After reading all of the rave reviews, I decided against my better judgement to buy this game. 
I first learnt of this game from playing the original V- Speedway game, which I love! Now admittedly I’ve literally only just got this game and have only played it for a couple of hours tops, so I’m still getting used to the controls which are exactly the same as V- Speedway except that the handbrake slides are zero drift because of front wheel drive. 
The one thing that concerned me in the reviews was how easy it was to get spun out by opponents, but the developers replied that they were working on that. I guess they’re still working on it because it’s really easy to get spun out still. The other thing I didn’t like about this game is that when you apply the brakes to come into a corner, the car locks up and gets sideways really easy. I understand that as with real driving you need to be off the brake before turning, and on the loud pedal (gas), but there’s such a thing as two footed driving where you can still “feather” the brakes while accelerating at the same time which allows you to come into corners a lot hotter, without locking up and getting sideways. 
Maybe I just got too used to the original version of this game and was enjoying the hand brake slides/drifting too much. Anyway hopefully the problems are more to do with me and I just need to play this game some more to  start to see the value. If that’s the case I’ll definitely come back and edit this  to five stars.

Update 01/29/2026
I’ve updated my rating to five stars. 
Now that I’ve been playing the game for a bit longer, and am now getting the hang of it it’s become very addictive. I started out on the beginner races, but they were really easy so I just kept going up until I wasn’t winning every race so easily (normal level). You still get spun out but that’s controllable if you’re not being a complete a/hole and trying to run others off the road on purpose. lol 
I tried the highest level and was getting completely smoked, but that was because I’m not up to that skill level yet and not because of faster cars or anything. 
I like that you can adjust the level of each race so you can get a feel for the tracks etc. as well. The prizes are higher percentage the higher the level you go as well. It’s great that there’s a way to watch other better players ghost cars  in time attack mode, so you can see where they’re braking and the racing lines they’re taking, and/or race their ghost car in a replay to get better. I used to play RR3 (Real Racing 3) and in time trials you could do this, and it helped improve my driving a lot. RR3 is not going to be available come March so this game will definitely be my new go to for racing fun. 
I hope the developers are going to add some real racetracks that aren’t street. Bathurst in Australia was one of my favourite tracks in RR3, along with Silverstone in England. I’m looking forward to what’s in store for the future of this game. 
It’s well worth the price and the more people that get into this, the better chance this game will be around for a long time. If you’re a racing fan there’s no better VR racing game out there, so get this and let’s keep it going for a long time!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the honest review! Just for clarity this is not a sequel to V-Speedway but a different game built almost from scratch, even if it shares a part of the design foundation. With that said, yes the car physics and the opponents AI are still a pain point for some, and will be revamped in update v1.5.x when we'll also add some new cars. I can assure physics and AI were even worse some updates ago, so we're making progress! 😅 I hope you'll stick around, we still have plenty to do with the game.

reviews.review_description:
Guys, you nailed it...this game rocks! I only have one small request- would it be possible to make the controllers rattle when you hit another car? That feeling would take this game to the next level. Right now they only rattle a bit from time to time with no apparent reasoning...unless I'm missing something. 
Seriously though... you guys know what you're doing and I'm excited to see what your next update is! Keep up the great work 👍

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the review! The "random" rattling comes from the car running over rougher patches of road; if you look at the asphalt, you will spot zones with more damaged surface and potholes!

reviews.review_description: You get exactly what you want out of it. Only lacking more cars, maps & a Ranked mode with a proper penalty system but that is also supposed to be coming I believe but what is here currently is already great.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We are working on additional content that we have planned for this year, hope you'll stick around for the upcoming updates!

reviews.review_description:
A bit of both!
Smooth and responsive controls.
Gripping the steering wheel is perfect!
Many game modes and even multiplayer. I love cruising around the town(that looks surprising good) in free mode.

Just a solid, fun game! Along with Exocars it’s the very best on Meta Quest

developer_response.body: Appreciate the review, thanks a lot! Gran Turismo is definitely a core inspiration for this game.

reviews.review_description: This game is very enjoyable seems to work really well fun can't wait until they add more stuff to it.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! You can check some of the planned updates on the recent News in the main menu, there's plenty in the works!

reviews.review_title: Best car racing game on standalone
reviews.review_description: This is one of those looks better in game than in videos and screenshots. There's so much detail in the environment, you can see interiors through the windows of buildings. I never played the version before the big update, but the physics handling of the cars is perfect. The ai is pretty aggressive, you can see them looking at you when you look at them and they'll remember you if you bump them! It's early access, 2 new environments and multiplayer on the way, but this is already a fantastic racing game with lots of attention to detail - even real-time mirrors and reflections on the helmet and interior. Blows my mind this runs on the quest standalone hardware to be honest.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! We hope you'll enjoy the upcoming updates, there's plenty we want to add to the game.

reviews.review_description: It's incredibly fun, I like it a lot. [Edit - this was original more rambling, edited for clarity] However, there's one specific sound effect that sounds really grating. It might be the sound of wind buffeting around your car. The Sennheisers I'm using aren't anywhere nearly as bad as something like a Skull Candy or a Beats by Dre, but they do still have a somewhat exaggerated bass response, and this sound effect [which I originally thought was the wind] sounds like nothing but overpowering, muddy, rumbling bass. Lots of popular headphones exaggerate the bass, so I think that should be taken into consideration. It'd be nice if that one specific sound effect were separated out from the rest of the car sounds so it could be turned off without also turning off other sounds (like tires squealing when you brake), or if it were rebalanced/EQ'd to be less bass heavy. This game has a great sense of "presence" and I like the way it looks aesthetically, I definitely prefer Downtown Club over other racing games on the Quest. This one specific sound effect is my only complaint, other than that this game is great. [Edit - response to the dev] After driving around a bit more, I might've been wrong about it being the wind. It might be the sound of driving over uneven/broken pavement. It's hard to identify what it's supposed to be because it's nothing but bass. Whatever it is, it goes away when the "car sounds" setting is turned all the way down. I'm sorry to complain, I tried to ignore it because the game overall is excellent, but I just can't ignore the auditory discomfort.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks a lot for the review, glad you are enjoying the game! We didn't encounter this specific issue about wind with our audio tests (we tried a bunch of different headsets but nothing particularly expensive, aside of Quest 2/3 integrated speakers), but thanks for the report. I'll make sure to add a wind noise setting in the Audio menu in the next available update, and check for the specific mixing values!

EDIT: ah! Yes that could definitely be the rumbling when going over uneven road and potholes. I’ll double check on that too!

reviews.review_title: Best car racer on the platform
reviews.review_description:
Its easily the best car racing game on quest. Its only one gig of space but looks soooo much better than "grid:legends" which is a whopping 30+ gigs of space. A quarter of most people's hard drives.

Pros: graphics
Ability to use joystick or hands for steering
Price
The sheer amount of content

Cons:
Having a shop to buy new cars set to a timer
The music is bad, turn it off put ur own on
The beginning cars are really slow, and are basically economy hatchback.

Definitely worth the price. Highly recommend. I waited on this one forever bc there is so much shovelware racers on quest. I assumed this was just another.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We put a lot of effort into this game building it from scratch to make sure it looks and feels unique. We have a lot planned for the future so we hope you'll look forward to it! PS: we love out Rock music. :P

reviews.review_description:
Great game with great potential, driving is great and is fun to drive around, Drifting isnt really possible but thats literally because all the cars are FWD, and as everyone should know, you cant drift a FWD.

The AI gets questionable though, One of my biggest complaints is that they constantly spin you out 
Get a good line and hit the apex perfectly? They dont care, you're spinning the next second.
And im not a fan of the ever so slight amount of rubberbanding for the AI, like sometimes ill take the same line, same gear and everything as the AI but they suddenly still creep away. Like i cant tell if im missing something or not.

Good game, but the AI is lacking

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We are aware about the AI and improving it is definitely on our todo list. The team is very small so we can only work on so much at any given time, right now multiplayer is the priority. But we have plenty of plans!

reviews.review_description:
its fun ... Very important in ones life 
but empty online

developer_response.body: Thanks! The game is still a bit niche, but we're continuing to support it and exit the Early Access soon. We hope more online players will arrive later!

reviews.review_description: This game is amazing but keep in mind this is a racing simulator not arcade. Keep up the great work and look forward to the future

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! We personally classify this as "simcade", because the car physics is somewhere in the middle between a simplified simulation and an arcade racer. Still, glad you liked it!

reviews.review_description: For me, this is the best driving experience on the Quest! The steering, the mirrors, the free roam, etc! ALL FIRE! 🔥

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! We put a lot of effort in this project and have more updates to come next. Hope you'll look forward to the future of the game!

reviews.review_description: I assume to win points you need to finish races within the top three....as i have the slowest car that is easy to shove off the road but does,nt have the ability to do that to others i,m beginning to consider deleting the app....any suggestions?

developer_response.body: Event races are limited to cars that can be competitive against each other. The "Normal" difficulty set by default is not necessarily simple, unless you already have some experience. We guarantee you can win all races with all cars at any difficulty once you are good enough. Lower the difficulty and start from there!

reviews.review_description: Just from watching the trailer made me purchase immediately 5 stars all day baby ill make an other review when I play

I love to just ride around free rid
e with my headphones on...traffic option on free ride would be nice...Great game.

developer_response.body: Glad you like it, thanks! The game is focusing mainly on racing so we have no plans for traffic at the moment, but we'll see in the future what can be done!

reviews.review_title: AN URBAN RACING GAME THAT POWERS VR!
reviews.review_description:
Downtown Club wowed me from the first spin.
The promise is kept: driving in VR with a realistic feeling of control.
From the very first laps, I appreciated the ergonomics of the controls—the virtual steering wheel, lever, and handbrake—which made the immersion more believable than in many arcade games.
The urban circuits, with buildings, tunnels, and parking lots, add style and visual diversity.
The game offers immersive VR racing where every turn, slide, or jump feels weighty and tense.
The mirrors are active, and the polished urban graphics (tunnels, skyscrapers, parking lots) are well-rendered and bring the city to life.
The "Free Drive" mode is a great way to explore the city without pressure.
Downtown Club could well become a benchmark for VR racing games, with its polished graphics and challenging circuits.
And online multiplayer mode is coming soon with update 1.3!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! The next update will be quite hefty so we hope you're looking forward to it!

reviews.review_description: It has really great potential. When i first spinned out i already started to get mad ai needs fixing need more cars and multiplayer other than that good game

developer_response.body: Thanks! The multiplayer is almost ready and can be tested already, more info on that on our blog. New cars will arrive after that, same for bot imorovements!

reviews.review_description: Downloaded the game to quest 3. Cannot review terms and conditions because wordpress error comes up on the browser.

developer_response.body: We aren't aware of any errors with our website, sorry. Since this is not an issue with the game (the game can still be started regardless) we'd appreciate if you could contact our support before leaving a review, so we can help you.

reviews.review_description: So good but add indicators would make it so much realistic and stuff in the car that we can interact with this would make it so much better

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! The game is focused on racing rather than everyday driving, but there will be more interactions later in the future!

reviews.review_description:
We. Need. More. Maps. And. Cars. PERIOD


Fire game btw

developer_response.body: Thanks! More cars and maps are coming later, you can check all the planned content in the roadmap!

reviews.review_description:
First and foremost I love the game and your other games as well but there are a couple things that need to be done:

1. NPC drivers need to stop spinning players out

2. Players need to be able to switch which thumbstick they use to turn using my left hand to turn inside of my right is weird to me because I'm right handed as are most people

3. You implemented a setting to override the automatic transmission to shift down but I'd love for you to implement the same setting although this time it overrides when you shift up and automatically shift down

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We know there's still a few points to improve, especially about CPU drivers, but we are still working hard on the game, our todo list is very long! I don't think I understand exactly the 3rd point, feel free to drop in our Discord to chat!

reviews.review_description:
...so it worths the price.
Using on Quest 2, set the resolution to 2048px, selected quality mode and still the performance is quite fluid. Especially in the tunnels. The opponent car details are something that my eyes are wondering during the game how cool that can look. 
So the frame rate using increased resolution is noticeably better in Downtown Club than is in V-Speedway Alpha. So a big congrats to the developer for achieving that. I don' think any other racing game could achieve this kind of performance with such nice visuals.

I would suggest for the developer if possible to add configuration to enable/disable the barriers. Removing/hiding barriers might even improve performance (just a guess) and could result in a more natural look of the city.  Even if the car would be bounced at the same points  (for simplification) when barriers are not seen the feeling of the driving still could be more natural to the eyes.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! V-Speedway was indeed just a test ground for us, we spent a lot of time making sure Downtown Club would look good, and I'd say we still have some way to go!

Track barriers were inspired by how real motorsport circuits are built inside city roads, so they are an integral part of the design... But there will be a new map later on with much more free air!

reviews.review_title: The best racing game on standalone BUTTT
reviews.review_description:
Downtown club has the potential to be the very best racing game on the quest platform. The handling is fantastic and the cars feel realistic once you get the settings right. The bots in this game are awful though. If you can pull ahead to first at the start you might have a chance. However, if you don't have a dramatic lead they will pit maneuver you over and over and over and over and over. Over and over and over and over and over. I'm trying to complete the first races on beginner and I'm struggling because they keep pit maneuvering me. Commuter games I really appreciate you guys and I happily purchased the supporter pack in v speedway but please improve the bots just a little bit.
Edit: 
thank you for the reply. I've gotten more used to it but I think the bots should avoid the back of your car better particularly around corners to avoid being spun out. 
Thank you for the fantastic game I can't wait for more updates

developer_response.body:
EDIT: thanks for the update, really appreciated!

ORIGINAL: Hey, we appreciate the feedback and we know opponents need some more tuning, but at Beginner difficulty they are much slower than you and will also wait for you if you crash- once you overtake them, keeping the lead should be very easy. Also make sure you are not closing on them leaving them no space to move- use the mirrors to check your surroundings.

Improvements to opponents are definitely on our todo list so we hope you'll look forward to those!

reviews.review_description: Been playing this game a long time and it is still really fun. Great mechanics ect just need more variety of cars and locations please. 🙏

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We are a super small studio so it will take some time, but new content is definitely in the making. Thanks for sticking with us!

reviews.review_title: Hands down the best VR racing on Quest
reviews.review_description: Incredible racing experience, the cars and tracks look great.  The steering wheel works perfectly, without the need for a fancy steering wheel accessory.  I've been trying to find a VR racing game to lose myself in, and this is it!  Now if only the AI wasn't so dedicated to screwing me over, every race...

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, appreciate it! AI improvements are definitely on the roadmap, there's still plenty we want to do with the game. Enjoy!

reviews.review_description: I love this game so much. It is so fun, but could you please consider adding Oval racing. I think it would be very fun especially when multiplayer comes out.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! We want to add proper motorsport circuits at some point so we will consider this! :)

reviews.review_description: The physics seems to be constantly being  improved and worked on. I play Assetto Corsa on my PC and this game on Quest 3 seems to be able to give the same feel in terms of weight transfers when braking, or accelerating, very good driving experience. AI seems to be better too.

developer_response.body: Thanks a ton for the flattering review!

reviews.review_description: First off the game is a little too expensive for it maybe if it was $8 it would be a good deal. and also the drifting is extremely hard to do. And i don't know if that is realistic or not but yea I mean it takes a long time to learn that. I hope you see this and fix something. EDITED. And I'm not saying it's bad or anything it's an amazing game and its potential is great. Maybe add something like you can get out of the car. Or.. add a new map like a NASCAR race track like Talladega super speedway you know that track right? Anyways great game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! The cars currently in the game are not drift cars, so the way they behave is intended- drift cars are coming next. We put a huge effort in the game quality and all future updates will be free, so we believe the game is worth the price. :)

reviews.review_description: Tire

developer_response.body: Thanks! 🚗🔥

reviews.review_title: The best racing game on the store.*
reviews.review_description:
The Meta store was almost entirely barren for years upon years when it came to racing games, and then along came DTC. A COUPLE released between '23 and '24 but lost their developers' support and in turn their communities, but this one managed to be a different case. 

It released, and still remains, in early access, yet manages to have better driving physics**, AI, visuals, cars in general, than pretty much any other game on the store right now (which is a shame to say, because there were 1 or 2 others which had just as much potential as this game does!). There's a pretty nice selection of content right off the bat for the moment, its' LONE DEVELOPER*** still cares more than ever for it, development seems to be slated towards more content and a multiplayer mode over the rest of the year, so there's pretty much nothing else like this out there with the promise that it currently holds. 

This is a good buy if you're even *mildly* interested in a racing/driving game, and there's probably no better time to do so (especially when small devs like this get such minimal support from the platform they give their games to). I can say that with good certainty, hopefully you'll be able to as well if you buy it.

*And the only currently supported one!
**Subjective opinion, of course, but there's not much to compare it to
***If I recall correctly, only one person made about 95% of this game other than someone else who models the cars for them, but I could be wrong.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your kind words, I can say for sure we're committed to the game regardless of the challenges this platform holds for us!

reviews.review_description:
this game been updated a few times since i last played it and its been improved loads.....
well done devs.......its a really beautiful racing game now with almost unNoticable bugs now👏👏👏

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We hope you're looking forward the upcoming updates!

reviews.review_description: This game has been amazing especially with only one person making it. It has been my favorite racing game on quest

developer_response.body: Thank you for supporting and appreciating our work!

reviews.review_description: I really like the game but I will never purchase a Early Access game again very frustrating waiting.

developer_response.body: I understand that, it’s as frustrating for the players as it is for our small team. With just one person working full time on the game, that’s the reality unfortunately!

reviews.review_description:
What a great racing game. From the graphics to the handling of the cars everything is a joy to play.
I'm having a blast!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Hope you're looking forward to the upcoming game updates!

reviews.review_description: As a member from the discord(Italiano), and a old player from past games. This game takes the cake, it has the graphics. And the most best map ever in a racing game. Once multiplayer comes out this game will peak.

developer_response.body: Thanks a ton! We hope to release multiplayer as soon as possible, can't wait to play with all the community!

reviews.review_description: The best vr racing game ever made! And I mean that!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We promise it will be even more worth soon enough. :)

reviews.review_description: Been playing for about a month and thought it was absolutely fantastic, just loaded it up and noticed i had the new update. Just wow everything is even better which i never thought was possible and like the new way you earn tickets to buy new things in the shop, before the update i just kept changing my gloves and car colour and didnt think id ever earn enough tickets to upgrade my car. Now i can and have and the sport version i have is like a little rocket😃 great work

developer_response.body: Thanks a ton, we're glad you liked the update! We will soon start to add new content to the game, hope you're looking forward to it!

reviews.review_description: For an early access, the game is in pretty good shape.  As others have mentioned, more locations would be great.  Some suggestions:  I found that sometimes the steering seemed to not track the controllers in hard turns.   Also, I noticed when adjusting the steering wheel to my desired position, the rear view mirror went down too low.  It would be nice to have a separate setting for controlling the mirrror height.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review and for the feedback! New locations are indeed coming later with free updates, together with new cars and more features. We are a super small team, but we're committed. :)

reviews.review_description: Honestly, i dont think i ever wrote a review before, but im so disappointed in this game, that i have to. I get it, its early access, but still, this is just straight up horrible. You brake? You slip, someone pushed your car a little bit? Slip, you accidentaly touched a car with your cars side? Slip. Its horrible, its like the road is made of ice, no friction what so ever. Its probably the best racing game thats available for native quest rn, but in my opinion, its almost unplayable in its state. I really do think their older games are so much better in almost every way, love V Speedway. Hope u will do something with the physics.

developer_response.body: Hey, the game has since been updated with major improvements to car physics, AI behavior, difficulty scale, and a lot more things. We would appreciate if you could give it a try and update the review accordingly.

reviews.review_title: First real driving game on Quest
reviews.review_description:
From the moment i purchased my quest 3 i was hoping i could find a driving game that felt realistic enough that i felt i was driving a real car. Sadly all i could find back then was cartoony driving games and the ones that were more realistic did not seem to cover that "realistic" itch. Then comes Downtown Club, what feels like the first real driving game on Quest as a whole. The driving feels amazing. I echo the other review that stated that driving on this game feels my driving a real car. There a huge level of polish in the driving mechanics in this game, you will absolutely be immersed by that as soon as you hop in. 

Honestly, with how realistic and polished the driving in this game is, i wish the devs went further into the driving sim aspect of the game, rather than the racing aspects. My personal wish for this game would be that the "Free ride" mode had an option to include traffic in the driving area, so you had to be mindful of other drivers, stop at red lights, and so on. The driving here feels good enough that it is almost practice. And adding an extra layer of what you'd have to be mindful for when driving irl would be amazing for people that just want to practice a bit, or just find relaxation in what driving in a city can be like. 

Final review, S-tier devs and S-tier game.

developer_response.body: Thanks you so much, highly appreciated! As you said we are curating the race aspect of the game for the time being, but who knows how things will be in the future! We hope you're looking forward to the next updates.

reviews.review_title: Best driving so far on standalone VR
reviews.review_description: I'm not traditionally a racing game guy, because I'm used to driving games that feel like they're almost trying to ram you into the wall. Cars that are fast, but so unstable you end up flying off the track a bunch until you learn the controls. Downtown Club's cars feel like driving MY car. You could really use this to practice driving IRL.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot, appreciate the review!

reviews.review_description:
This really is an excellent arcade SIM racing game and especially since the update to v1.1

The game is so much fun now going nose to nose with other cars in the pack and enjoying the experience, and the update has made it much more user-friendly as the cars handle better and are more forgiving for the novice driver.

With multi-player in the pipeline this deserves to go viral once that happens...

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! Multiplayer is definitely the next big thing we're going to work on. We cannot wait to race against our players! :P

reviews.review_description:
MARCH 2025 UPDATE:  Though still enjoying the game, I am starting to find it limited after a few sessions. It feels like I am running through the same 3-5 tracks (that are already similar given the city setting of the game),  that play the same 2-3 songs, and racing in and against the same 3 cars (with the 'Sports' versions of these cars being locked to the very last 5% of the single player events only, sadly, and also the only versions to have the cool paintjobs for some reason).
I would say there is not enough different Events to complete as well, but as is there isn't enough content to carry more anyway, so I get that.
It's a racing sim-cade title that's likely made with the expectation of multiplayer racing being a draw for it's replayability like many other titles.
I've also found that I hate how the Shop functions on a timer. Considering the only way to get tickets is completing 3-race Events or 1 10-minute Endurance race, you better hope you have time to complete enough events to get the items you want before they are switched out (something life doesn't always allow for many on most days). This especially hurts if its a new car type needed to even continue new Events.
So I'd rather have progressive unlocks or just everything in-store at once without a timer. Though maybe I'm just missing the point of this design.
I've also encountered so many moments of easily being spun out since my last review (I'm talking full uncontrollable 180s) just from hitting a wall (Inconsistently happens) and other cars randomly making me lose control from touching me (I tried that with them, but I'M the one that spun out from touching THEM, while they remained fine. 😅Nothing spins them out, so it only seems like the player is on ice at times and I've learned not to race aggressively.). Overall this is manageable enough and better than it used to be though (on Beginner-Easy anyway. The game is impossible for me past that in part due to this because there is no getting away from opponent cars on higher difficulties, so likely just a skill issue. 😅).
So though as a solo player only gamer this game is starting to lose its luster a bit (no issue given the few hours of fun had), I still think it's great and very well done (especially given the small team at work and still being in Early Access), so my previous level of recommendation hasn't changed even if I wished the Shop mechanics were changed and there were more cars.

FEB. 2025: After the most recent updates to the game, the game is FINALLY playable even for my disabled legally-blind self (even if I still keep it at Beginner... 😅). So it feels good for someone who hasn't been able to drive in real life  for 10 years.
On the easiest difficulty setting, I can actually win races and full events easy enough so long as I am not messing up (it'll get tougher, I know) and outside of the rare moments where I seem to spin out with nothing around me (that I seen at least), any races I don't get 1st place in feels like MY fault now. On those occasions, I feel ready to do better next time and am actually enjoying myself doing it!
With this, I have been able to start learning the cars and how to control everything as needed now that everything seems to feel better and not frustrating me/ feeling impossibly unfair (for me). I feel I'll actually get better now. 
So after 2 or so hours of FINALLY getting somewhere for the first time since I've had this game on release day- I am loving it!
I can see the great work that went into everything and making the game feel as good as it does (though I always did, but had trouble appreciating it all and then throwing a temper tantrum...😅).
So now I'm with everyone else who says this is the best racing title on the platform and wholeheartedly recommend it now!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We are happy the game is finally enjoyable for you, and we'll keep improving it as much as we can in the future. Hope you'll look forward to the next updates!

reviews.review_title: By far the best Racing Game on the Quest
reviews.review_description:
I bought Downtown Club a long time ago and was very impressed with the graphics and driving experience. However, the car swerved far too quickly and you spun around completely, which was very frustrating.

A new patch has been out for a few weeks now that has completely fixed the problem and also improved the game overall. It's still early access, but in my opinion it's much more mature than 90% of the Quest games I usually have. The virtual steering wheel works great and the races are almost always exciting.

In any case, I can highly recommend it and recommend it to anyone who is looking for a decent VR racing game or to be honest: the best racing game for the Quest.

developer_response.body: Appreciate the review, we're happy the Core Update improved the experience so much. Hope you're looking forward to the next updates this year!

reviews.review_description: The Devs have worked really hard and the latest update has transformed the car control which was my only real gripe at launch. The car models have been buffed recently and this game has working wing and rear view mirrors (see Grid Legends, it can be done!) This is a must buy game. Can't wait to see what the future updates will bring.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot, we appreciate that! We’ll do our best to bring new content to the game now that the Core Update is out.

reviews.review_title: Best Racing Game On Quest Standalone
reviews.review_description:
This is the best racing game I tried on standalone VR, the graphics are nice compared to their previous game "V-Speedway" which I still play sometimes,  the gameplay is good, you can adjust the steering wheel rotation etc, I'm waiting for the multiple mode which I wish is gonna be added soon. I want to suggest you some features and options.

 - Add limits to the car body, when I try to touch the car windows or anything my hand and body go through it, it will be nice if we can touch the interior like 

 - We can use the screen to access to web browser or just to change some settings. 

 - Free drive is good but if we can add some cars so we can feel we are in the city driving and chilling. 

 - I don't know why but I always have problems with the gear even if I tried to change the sensitivity many time (I don't have this problem with V-speedway)

- We can click on many buttons also like (mudrunners)

- We can also fix handbrake on (OFF)

- Sometimes I feel the car needs more weight especially when other cars hit me.

Thank you for this game 🎮 🤍

developer_response.body: Thanks to you for the review and the suggestions! We have a long way to go with this game and always listen to all feedback, hope you'll look forward the next updates!

reviews.review_description:
This was one of the first apps I installed after purchasing the Quest in Nov. 2024 curious about how a driving/racing sim feels in VR. 
First impressions were ok. The only real problem that I found was that the steering was a little janky at times. Partly because of my own ineptitude of steering without holding a physical wheel and partly because the software got confused after my car got nudged and spun out by an opponent.
The V1.1 update was a genuine game changer, the vehicle and steering feels much more planted and I feel that I am more in control. Thanks devs. The in-game economy has also been greatly improved. 
I have been driving for over five decades so I know what a vehicle should feel like and considering I'm getting pretty little feedback from the car itself, this app is very good and I can recommend it. 
If I had a wishlist (which I do) a horn would be nice (nothing wrong with a bit of road rage and another button to press) and a garage where I can view my shiny new cars. 
I'm looking forward to seeing how this develops further but (pun intended) it's on the right track.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much, we're happy the 1.1 update got things on the right track for you! There's still plenty of roadmap ahead of us (new cars, new maps, multiplayer, etc), so we'll be busy on this game for much more. Hope you'll stick around! :D

reviews.review_title: The best driving game on Quest
reviews.review_description:
From the second you jump in you can see the quality just oozes throughout the game.
Amazing in car graphics to open the game, the tutorial starts you gently and eases you nicely into the race mode.

The AI cars look awesome and provide a great challenge even with n the easier race tracks.
I’m not far in but with every race I feel I’m getting better at my  race strategy.
Full marks to the devs, I’m stunned it’s still early access.
Simply brilliant.

developer_response.body: Thanks a ton for the review! We are currently running an optional Beta version for all players where you can try a lot of upcoming improvements to the core game. Hope you're looking forward to the next updates!

reviews.review_description:
I loved V speedway and I love this game even more. The tracks are fun, the racing is fun, the cars are fun and OMG driving over the curbs might be the most satisfying thing ever. I would recommend this game to anyone, even if you’re not a fan of Motorsports. I also think you guys found the perfect balance of sim racing and arcade racing. 
If I could choose anything else to add (that’s not already on the roadmap) I would choose to add NASCAR/Stock racing, like add a stock car and then add either a short banked track or a Super Speedway or both🙏. The driving might be a little different like how it could be more focused on drafting and pack racing but I Think it’s another part of Motorsport I would like to try in vr. 
Apart from that I can’t wait for GT cars to get added aswell as multiplayer. Thanks so much for this masterpiece of a game.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review, we really appreciate it. NASCAR/Stock is not on the roadmap yet but once we complete all the features listed in the Early Access roadmap, we'll be able to think about other racing categories such as that!

reviews.review_description: Finally, a driving game that actually works well on Quest 3 standalone.   The first driving game I haven't refunded.  Wow, loving it!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks, that means a lot! We have many things coming next. :)

reviews.review_title: Best standalone sim racer on quest 3
reviews.review_description: As the title says and by the way if you get motion sickness just play in short sessions mine disappeared after a week so stick with it !!

developer_response.body: Thanks you so much! The game also features some comfort options to help with motion sickness, just in case!

Thank you so much, too kind! The Beta for the update you mentioned will be live in the next hours so owners of the game will be able to test it before it goes public. There's a lot we want to do with this game so I hope you'll look forward to the updates!

reviews.review_description: Really good racing game, and still in early access. Can't wait to see how this develops

developer_response.body: Thanks! Hope you're looking forward to future updates! :)

reviews.review_description:
Downtown Club is a testament to the dedication and passion of its developer. Having known them for years, I’ve witnessed firsthand the love and effort poured into this project daily. The gameplay is smooth, the cars are an absolute joy to drive, and every course invites you to chase records. This game is pure fun while providing a very welcome challenge. 
If you haven’t played it yet, you’re missing out.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, we are definitely pouring our love and commitment into this project. I hope you'll look forward the next updates, we have a lot going on behind the scenes!

reviews.review_description: Okay man the game is ultra realistic and you have to play events to get a surprise if you are on 1st, 2nd and 3rd place.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! :D

reviews.review_description: If you wanna drive cars, you need to get this game I’d love this steering wheel and the people in everything. The nighttime in the sunniness is the best. The cars are the best. They’re like real people.

developer_response.body: Thanks a ton for the review! We are working hard to make the game even better than it is. :)

reviews.review_description: "I really loved the game! The 900-degree steering wheel and manual gear shifting add great authenticity. It shows that when someone puts their heart into their work, amazing things happen. While Oculus is essentially a mobile device, the game looks fantastic on the VR hardware, proving that talent matters too.I hope to see future expansions with new tracks, car types, and racing competitions. Driving through the streets of a small town with traffic, where you need to get from point A to point B in the shortest time possible, or having a Need for Speed-style race would be awesome! This could provide ongoing revenue for the developer and motivate them to keep improving the game. Bravo, and keep up the great work! Racing simulators are an underdeveloped niche in Oculus VR games.This is a translation done with AI, so I apologize if anything is unclear. Thank you!"

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review, I really appreciate that!
